title: Nothing Within the Room of Gaff
display_title: Nothing Within the Room of Gaff
wikitext:
[]: Nothing Within the Room of Gaff             Quote Post by VoidEater » Tue Jan 27, 2009 7:50 pm Ep 23' - all the Rei-fish are empty vessels, there were no more souls to inhabit them: Ritsuko: There was nothing within the room of Gaff. * * Variously spelled in other sources, but basically the "waiting room" for souls to be born on Earth in Judaic thought. Was this literal, and indeed have there been no recent births? Or was Ritsuko waxing poetic, ironic, in her dismissal of the vessels? Toji has a younger sister, and Hikari perhaps (IIRC), but I suppose these Reis may have been harvested after that... If indeed the room is empty, that implies two things: that Lilith had a finite number of souls to birth, and that souls upon death do not migrate back to Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jan 27, 2009 8:10 pm The one thing I know for sure is that Chamber of Gaf are those vulva-palms of Lilith's we see in EoE. I believe Ritsuko's line in episode 23' however referenced 2I. Probably was explained somewhere here: http://evacommentary.org/episode-23/23_reiquarium.html Hope that helps.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jan 27, 2009 9:33 pm With all the genital imagery, blatant sex scenes and euphamisms floating about in EOE, I'm honestly suprised 01 turned into a tree/nerve cluster/3D-cross thingy rather than just a giant space dong.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 27, 2009 9:37 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: The one thing I know for sure is that Chamber of Gaf are those vulva-palms of Lilith's we see in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Tue Jan 27, 2009 10:51 pm So is Lilith herself 'the chamber' or do the slits in her hand act as some interdimensional teleporter that leads to the real chamber. I always thought the black egg was supposed to be the chamber, but I guess that doesn't make sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Jan 27, 2009 11:04 pm ^ I think that the egg (black moon) inter-dimensionally teleporting from Guf through GNR's hands sounds about right to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 27, 2009 11:23 pm The show itself kind of muddles the issue... - After the Black Moon is exposed: SEELE 11: First, return the Geofront... Keel: To its true form. (Pull-back shot of the Earth and Lilith's Egg) Fuyutsuki (off screen): The egg of Lilith, the source of human life: The Black Moon. I have no desire to return to that empty shell. (on screen) But even that depends on the will of Lilith.
